summary: Fixed image upload authentication bug. Root cause: Frontend was trying to read token from localStorage but app uses httpOnly cookies. Fixed handleImageUpload to use withCredentials:true instead of manually passing Bearer token.

issues: Some products show '$NaN' for price due to inconsistent price format in database (pre-existing issue)

critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIX: handleImageUpload was using localStorage.getItem('token') but app uses httpOnly cookies for auth
Changed to use withCredentials:true which automatically sends cookies
Removed unnecessary token check that was causing 'Not authenticated' error
Added redirect to login page on 401 errors for better UX

success_rate.backend: 100% - All 6 image upload auth tests passed
success_rate.frontend: 100% - Image upload and product save working

context_for_next_testing_agent: Image upload authentication bug is FIXED. The issue was that handleImageUpload was trying to read token from localStorage but the app uses httpOnly cookies. Changed to use withCredentials:true which automatically sends cookies with the request. All image upload tests pass.

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: 'Not authenticated' error when trying to save a product with uploaded image
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Frontend handleImageUpload function was checking localStorage.getItem('token') which doesn't exist - app uses httpOnly cookies for authentication
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Removed localStorage token check, using withCredentials:true to automatically send httpOnly cookies
bug_fix_verification.verification_result: FIXED - Image upload now works correctly with cookie-based authentication

rca_of_the_issue.symptom: User sees 'Not authenticated' error when uploading image in Products Manager
investigation: 1. Checked backend logs - saw 401 Unauthorized for /api/upload/image
2. Reviewed ProductsManager.jsx - found handleImageUpload checking localStorage.getItem('token')
3. Reviewed AuthContext.jsx - confirmed app uses httpOnly cookies, not localStorage
4. Verified backend accepts both cookies and Bearer token for auth
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Mismatch between auth mechanism: Backend sets httpOnly cookies on login, but handleImageUpload was looking for token in localStorage which was never set
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Changed handleImageUpload to use withCredentials:true (sends cookies automatically) instead of manually passing Bearer token from localStorage
rca_of_the_issue.prevention: Ensure all API calls in the app consistently use withCredentials:true for cookie-based auth
